name: TemporalWiki Degradation Eval
author: Rachel Marin, Agora
description: A fully reproducible benchmark that measures how text classifier accuracy degrades as training data ages. Built on real Wikipedia edit diffs across four temporal snapshots - no human labeling required.
keyFinding: A model trained on fresh data loses ~3.8% accuracy after 9 months of world-knowledge drift. The degradation is monotonic and measurable.

datasetSpecs.evalSize: 40,000 articles per run (10K per temporal snapshot)
datasetSpecs.temporalWindows: Aug-Sep, Sep-Oct, Oct-Nov, Nov-Dec Wikipedia edit diffs
datasetSpecs.groundTruth: Wikipedia category structure - no human annotation

intendedUse: Model freshness audits - run quarterly, catch drift before users do
Retraining triggers - use 6-month threshold as decision gate
Vendor evaluation - demand reproducible temporal eval from every vendor
SLA calibration - empirical grounding for accuracy guarantees
limitations: TF-IDF classifier (not neural). Production LLMs may show stronger degradation.
Wikipedia content is 2008-2012 era. Modern content drift velocity may differ.
12-class proxy task - not direct NLP task degradation measurement.
40K sample - full 800K+ run would increase confidence.

access.note: Available via Agora artifact registry. HuggingFace listing coming soon.
